title: dalbergia mollis var. menabeensis
intro: Dalbergia mollis var. menabeensis, also known as the Menabe Dalbergia or Menabe Rosewood, is a plant species native to Madagascar, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Dalbergia mollis var. menabeensis include relief from fever, pain, and inflammation, as well as its potential use as an antibacterial agent to treat skin infections. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infused oil, and its bark is sometimes used to make a powder for medicinal purposes.
